Based on the search results, here are the key OpenAI API updates for 2026:

## Model Releases and Updates

GPT-5.2 was released as the newest flagship model in the GPT-5 model family, showing improvements over GPT-5.1. What's new in GPT-5.2 is a new xhigh reasoning effort level, concise reasoning summaries, and new context management using compaction.

OpenAI optimized its inference stack for API customers, with GPT-5.2 and GPT-5.2-Codex now running approximately 40% faster.

GPT-5.2-Codex was released to the Responses API as a version of GPT-5.2 optimized for agentic coding tasks.

The new real-time model gpt-realtime-1.5 and its accompanying audio model have been officially released.

## API Feature Updates

Batch API is now supported for GPT Image models: gpt-image-1.5, chatgpt-image-latest, gpt-image-1, and gpt-image-1-mini.

Server-side compaction was launched in the Responses API.

Support for Skills in the Responses API was launched, supporting both local execution and hosted container-based execution.

Thread forking into sub-agents is now available, making it easier to branch work without leaving the current conversation.

## Deprecations

The Realtime API Beta will be deprecated and removed from the API on March 24, 2026.

OpenAI is scheduled to deprecate DALL-E-3 on May 2, 2026.

The Assistants API beta will sunset on August 26, 2026.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

Based on the search results, here are the recent Bun runtime updates in 2026:

## Major Release: Bun v1.3

Bun has released version 1.3, marking its biggest release yet with full-stack development capabilities, unified database APIs, and performance improvements across the runtime. Bun 1.3 revolutionizes full-stack JavaScript development with unified database APIs and zero-config frontend setup, with enhanced performance including built-in Redis support and optimized bundling.

## Key Features Added

Bun 1.3 expands package management with dependency catalogs for monorepos, isolated installs are now the default for workspaces, and includes a new bun update --interactive command for selective dependency updates along with bun why to explain dependency chains.

The release introduces a built-in Redis client that delivers more than 7.9 times the performance of the popular ioredis package, with all standard Redis operations supported.

## Recent Minor Updates

Version 1.3.6 (released January 27, 2026) includes Bun.Archive API for creating & extracting tarballs with gzip compression, Bun.JSONC for parsing JSON with comments, metafile & files options in Bun.build for bundle analysis and virtual files, Response.json() 3.5x faster, 15% faster async/await, 30% faster Promise.race, 20x faster Bun.hash.crc32, and faster Buffer operations with SIMD.

Version 1.3.5 (released January 13, 2026) added Bun.Terminal API, compile-time feature flags, and improved Bun.stringWidth accuracy.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

content.text:
Here's a quick summary:

**OpenAI API** — GPT-5.2 and GPT-5.2-Codex are the latest models, ~40% faster. New features include server-side compaction, Skills in the Responses API, and thread forking. Assistants API sunsetting Aug 2026; DALL-E-3 deprecating May 2026.

**Bun Runtime** — Bun 1.3.6 is the latest, featuring built-in Redis client (7.9x faster than ioredis), `Bun.Archive` API, 3.5x faster `Response.json()`, 15% faster async/await, and SIMD-accelerated Buffer ops.

**Bash** — `sleep 6 && echo done` completed successfully.

Sources:
- [OpenAI API Changelog](https://developers.openai.com/api/docs/changelog)
- [OpenAI Release Notes](https://releasebot.io/updates/openai)
- [Bun Releases](https://github.com/oven-sh/bun/releases)
- [Bun v1.3.1 - InfoQ](https://www.infoq.com/news/2026/01/bun-v3-1-release/)
- [Bun Blog](https://bun.com/blog)
